📖 Study Workflow
📝 Textbook: Quiz
  1. Set goal — decide how many questions before starting.
  2. Write answers — close the book, answer from recall only.
  3. Read the answer — compare against the text, note gaps.
  4. AI grammar check — paste into Claude project, highlight strong vocab for report writing.
  5. Convert to Q&A — reframe key facts as question-answer pairs for notes or Anki.
🔁 Revision: Re-read highlights and Q&A at scheduled intervals.
📚 Textbook: Reading
  1. Outline + images first — scan structure and all figures before any prose. Prime the schema.
  2. Flag uncertain pages — mark page numbers where you're unsure or blank. Don't read yet.
  3. Brainstorm — for each flagged page, write what you think the content should say.
  4. Correct flagged pages — now read those pages, fill gaps, fix errors in your brainstorm.
🔁 Revision: Q&A from flagged pages at scheduled intervals.
📄 Paper
  1. Outline + images first — read abstract, headings, figures, and tables before the body text.
  2. Flag uncertain sections — note figure numbers or sections you can't interpret yet.
  3. Brainstorm — write what you expect those sections to say or show.
  4. Correct flagged sections — read the actual content, reconcile against your brainstorm.
🔁 Revision: Q&A using Claude project at scheduled intervals.
🎓 Lecture
  1. Read the outline — get the big picture before watching or attending.
  2. Make questions or Anki cards — for each key point, including image-based cases.
🔁 Revision: Review those questions at scheduled intervals.
⏱ Spaced repetition intervals: D0 → D1 → D3 → D7 → D14 → D30 → D90 → D180
